executionHistory.summary: Initial business analysis and spec.md creation for government alerts connector
Chaos-hardening: found and fixed 8 issues (3 race conditions, 1 memory leak, 2 input validation gaps, 1 error handling gap, 1 context handling gap). Added 8 chaos tests.

Chaos-hardening R26: found and fixed 3 issues (TravelProvider location validation bypass C-017-001, notifier panic crash C-017-002, Close-during-Sync health corruption C-017-003). Added 9 adversarial tests.
Stabilize R56: found and fixed 1 issue (STAB-017-001 missing maxSyncDuration bound for aggregate sync timeout). Added 2 adversarial tests. 168 total test functions.

Reconcile R72: full claimed-vs-implemented reconciliation. All functional claims substantiated. 168 tests pass. No code drift, no config wiring gaps. 2 info-level documentation observations (design single-file drift accepted, report test count snapshot staleness noted). No changes needed.

Spec-review pass for legacy reconcile-to-doc completion. Verified spec.md, design.md, scopes.md, and report.md remain mutually consistent after Apr 22 harden sweep (175 test functions, 7 sources wired end-to-end with proximity_verified flag). No active superseded specs. No redundancy with adjacent connector specs (016-weather, 019-connector-wiring). Spec content remains authoritative for the implemented government-alerts surface.

Chaos re-validation: ran -race over TestConcurrentSyncHealth, TestConcurrentCloseHealth, TestSyncContextCancellation, TestSync_MaxSyncDurationBoundsTimeout, TestSync_ParentContextCancellation_StopsSyncEarly, TestKnownMapEviction. All clean (1.677s). C-017-001/002/003 and STAB-017-001 fixes still hold under race detector.

Trace-guard cleanup batch (post MIT-FRAMEWORK-001 patch): closed 18 trace failures (18->0). Added Test Plan tables to Scopes 01-06 referencing internal/connector/alerts/alerts_test.go (already mentioned in report.md). Added 'Scenario "<scenario name>": ' DoD-trace prefixes to existing DoD bullets covering SCN-GA-PROX-001, SCN-GA-LIFE-001, SCN-GA-USGS-001/002, SCN-GA-NWS-001, SCN-GA-CONN-001, SCN-GA-TSUN-001, SCN-GA-AQI-001, SCN-GA-GDACS-001, SCN-GA-NOTIF-001/002. No DoD content rewriting; no source/test/config/framework changes. artifact-lint clean; traceability-guard PASSED 0 failures.

Simplify-to-doc (stochastic sweep-2026-05-23-r30 Round 2, parent-expanded child workflow): SIMP-017-001 fixed. Extracted claimAlert(id, now) bool helper to consolidate 7 inline dedup-acquire patterns (Lock/check known[id]/record/Unlock) in Sync() across all 7 source loops (USGS earthquake/volcano, NWS, NOAA tsunami, InciWeb wildfire, AirNow AQI, GDACS). Behavior-preserving (defer Unlock matches original critical-section boundary). LOC 1815->1788. Race detector clean: TestSync_Deduplication, TestSync_ConcurrentWithLiveKnownMapWrites, TestKnownMapEviction, TestConcurrentSyncHealth, TestConcurrentCloseHealth all pass. Full alerts suite green under -race (4.953s). go build ./... clean. No spec content changed; certification preserved.

Regression-to-doc (stochastic sweep-2026-05-23-r30 Round 12, parent-expanded child workflow): REG-017-001 found and fixed via BUG-017-002. Framework upgrade on 2026-05-12 (commit 3037eb8c) tightened traceability-guard.sh G068 matcher (word-length floor 4->3, trimmed stop list). Under v3.8.0, Scope 03 SCN-GA-NWS-002 lost faithful DoD mapping (existing bullets scored 2/3 required overlap and lacked trace ID). Artifact-only fix: inserted 1 scenario-prefix DoD bullet for SCN-GA-NWS-002 into scopes.md Scope 03 between existing 'Event types classified' and '17 unit tests pass' bullets. All other bullets byte-identical. Underlying behavior (mapNWSSeverity + classifyNWSEventType) was already delivered since 2026-04-11 and continuously tested. Post-fix: traceability-guard PASS (13/13 mapped, 0 unmapped), artifact-lint PASS (parent + bug), go test PASS (175 tests, 7.358s), race subset clean (1.334s). Spec status preserved (done); certification preserved.
